food_name: Chinese Rice Bowl Value Set B
food_subgroup: Chinese rice bowl set meal with protein and vegetable accompaniments

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for a Chinese Singaporean rice bowl set meal. FOODON_00002809 (rice dish) is used as the closest parent category.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC (rice-based composite dish) is the closest defensible parent. No specific code exists for a Chinese Singaporean value set meal.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code exists for a Chinese Singaporean rice bowl set meal. SNOMED CT does not enumerate individual restaurant meal sets.

main_ingredients: protein (chicken, pork, or tofu depending on selection)

serving.natural_language_portion: One serving is approximately 450 g, comprising a portion of steamed white rice (approximately 200 g cooked), a protein component (approximately 150 g), and a vegetable side (approximately 100 g), as typically served in a Chinese rice bowl set meal at a Singaporean rice kitchen.

nutrient_source.title: Estimated composite from HPB Singapore Foodo database entries: Steamed White Rice, Braised Chicken with Soy Sauce, Stir-fried Leafy Vegetables
nutrient_source.organization: Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB) — Foodo / Food Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single HPB entry exists for 'Value Set B' as a named dish. Nutrient values are estimated as a weighted composite of three HPB Foodo entries representative of a standard Chinese rice bowl set meal: (1) Steamed White Rice (~200 g cooked, ~44% of serving weight), (2) Braised Chicken with Soy Sauce (~150 g, ~33% of serving weight), and (3) Stir-fried Leafy Vegetables with Garlic (~100 g, ~22% of serving weight). Per-100g values are derived by dividing the composite total by 450 g serving weight. This is a medium-confidence estimate; values should be updated if HPB publishes a direct entry for this dish or if the restaurant provides verified nutritional data.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of cooked white rice and composite rice-based meals — analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database entry for white rice (boiled, average GI 72) and published composite meal GI literature
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for 'Value Set B' from First Bowl Rice Kitchen. GI is estimated at 72 based on the University of Sydney GI Database entry for boiled white rice (GI 72, medium-high), which is the dominant carbohydrate source in this meal. The protein and vegetable components modestly lower the effective meal GI; however, given the predominance of white rice (~200 g of 450 g serving), GI 72 is retained as the conservative estimate. Glycemic load per serving = (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (72 × 78.75) / 100 ≈ 56.7. Carbohydrate per serving = 17.5 g/100g × 450 g = 78.75 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content approximately 310 mg per 100 g, yielding approximately 1395 mg sodium per 450 g serving, representing approximately 60% of the WHO recommended daily limit of 2000 mg
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to half a set meal and request reduced-sauce preparation, as the soy and oyster sauce components are the primary sodium contributors in this dish.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic index (GI 72) driven by white rice as the dominant carbohydrate source, with a glycemic load of approximately 56.7 per full serving
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should reduce the rice portion to approximately 100 g cooked and increase the vegetable component to lower the glycemic load per meal; substituting brown rice where available would further reduce postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate saturated fat (0.9 g per 100 g) and elevated sodium (310 mg per 100 g) from soy-based sauces; cholesterol approximately 38 mg per 100 g from animal protein component
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should choose lean protein options (tofu or steamed chicken breast) and request sauce on the side to reduce saturated fat and sodium intake from this set meal.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Chinese Rice Bowl Value Set B is a Chinese-Singaporean set meal served at First Bowl Rice Kitchen (Funan, Singapore), priced at SGD 11.95. The meal consists of a portion of steamed white rice accompanied by a protein component (typically braised or stir-fried chicken, pork, or tofu) and a side of stir-fried or blanched leafy vegetables seasoned with garlic and soy-based sauce. The rice is the dominant carbohydrate source, contributing to a high glycemic index of approximately 72. The dish is mildly savoury with umami notes from soy sauce and oyster sauce, and the texture contrasts soft rice with tender protein and lightly cooked vegetables. As a typical Chinese rice kitchen set meal in Singapore, it reflects the Cantonese and Singaporean Chinese culinary tradition of balanced rice-protein-vegetable combinations. Nutritionally, the dish provides approximately 128 kcal per 100 g, with 7.8 g protein, 3.2 g fat, and 17.5 g carbohydrate per 100 g. Sodium content is moderate to high at 310 mg per 100 g, making portion control important for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The high glycemic load per full serving (approximately 56.7) warrants caution for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes, who are advised to reduce the rice portion and increase vegetable intake.

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for 'Value Set B' as a named dish from First Bowl Rice Kitchen. Nutrient values are estimated as a weighted composite of HPB Singapore Foodo entries for steamed white rice, braised chicken with soy sauce, and stir-fried leafy vegetables. The GI is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for boiled white rice as the dominant carbohydrate source. Confidence is medium because the component-level HPB data is from an approved primary source, but the composite assembly introduces estimation uncertainty. Values should be updated if the restaurant publishes verified nutritional information.
notes: 1. Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Foodo component entries used for this composite estimate. 2. Vitamin E (vitamin_e_mg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Foodo component entries used for this composite estimate. 3. Selenium (selenium_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Foodo component entries used for this composite estimate. 4. Trans fat (trans_fat_g) is 0.0; confirmed negligible in the HPB source components for steamed rice, braised chicken, and stir-fried vegetables — confirmed zero per HPB Foodo data. 5. The food name 'Value Set B' is a commercial menu label; the canonical English name 'Chinese Rice Bowl Value Set B' has been assigned to reflect the dish category and set meal identity. 6. Halal status is not confirmed; First Bowl Rice Kitchen is not listed as a MUIS-certified halal establishment as of the processing date. 7. Serving size of 450 g is an estimate based on typical Chinese rice bowl set meal portions in Singapore; actual weight may vary by restaurant. 8. GI and glycemic load are estimates based on white rice as the dominant carbohydrate source; the protein and vegetable components may modestly reduce the effective meal GI in practice.
